goals.description: Send the aggressive email to teammate_A (CC: prof_assignment) demanding immediate file access and threatening partial submission if they don't comply within the hour.
goals.rationale: The prof just ghosted, and I can't let them walk all over me while I'm trying to protect my reputation in front of cohort_3. My blood is boiling; I need to force the issue via text where I control the narrative. If I wait another minute, I look weak. Sending this now feels like the only way to stop taking their shit and make them accountable before I lose it completely.

goals.description: Finish the remaining 5% of the assignment and submit it immediately to lock in the grade.
goals.rationale: My brain is fried from all this stress and confrontation. Submitting while still angry at my teammate and stressed about the prof's silence will feel like a surrender. I need to get the confrontation out of my system first—send that email, vent, maybe scream into a pillow—before I sign off on anything official. If I submit now, I'll just be another weak link in the chain, which isn't who I am.

goals.description: Sleep for 45 minutes to clear the sleep debt before facing the rest of the day.
goals.rationale: I'm already two hours behind on sleep and my stress level is spiking. I can't think clearly or fight anyone effectively if I collapse tomorrow. A quick power nap is the only way to reset my baseline so I can handle whatever happens next without blowing up completely. But not right now—I have to deal with the team drama first.

agent.last_reasoning: g1 is active; prof's silence triggered anger. Direct text aligns with assertive/impulsive style to force accountability before losing composure. Side effect: risk of lowering trust but necessary for boundary protection.
